question.text: What is the maximum NFO period for an ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme)?

answer.text: ELSS is exempt from the 15-day maximum NFO period

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** While most mutual fund schemes have a maximum NFO period of 15 days, ELSS schemes are exempt from this limit. ELSS can keep their NFO open for a longer period. This exception exists because ELSS is a tax-saving instrument with a specific investment purpose linked to the financial year.
**The Trap:** The minimum 3-day NFO period still applies to ELSS. Only the maximum period cap of 15 days is relaxed. Students sometimes incorrectly think ELSS is completely exempt from all NFO rules.
**Cross-Exam Context:** ELSS comes with a mandatory 3-year lock-in period, which is the shortest among all Section 80C investment options. The relaxation of NFO period for ELSS was to give investors more time to subscribe to these tax-saving schemes.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While ELSS NFOs may have longer subscription periods, educate clients that investing in an existing ELSS scheme at NAV is no different from investing during an NFO at face value. Do not use the longer NFO window as a sales pitch — the tax benefit and lock-in period are identical regardless of when the investment is made.
